summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Traefik v1.7.34 exposes the full routing configuration via the /api endpoint without authentication. An unauthenticated curl request returns all backend server URLs, frontend routing rules, and internal service topology.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should start Traefik with a sample configuration containing internal backend URLs, then demonstrate that an unauthenticated GET /api request returns the full configuration including those internal URLs.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, started Traefik v1.7.34 with sample backends at 10.0.0.50:3000 and 192.168.1.100:8080, and the unauthenticated curl to /api returned the complete JSON configuration including both internal backend URLs.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sends an unauthenticated GET request to http://localhost:8080/api, checks that the response is valid JSON, and confirms it contains the expected internal backend URLs (10.0.0.50:3000 and 192.168.1.100:8080) that should not be accessible without authentication.
evidence_produced_by_run: The API response is valid JSON containing the full routing configuration.
The response includes backend server URLs with internal IP addresses (10.0.0.50:3000, 192.168.1.100:8080).
No authentication headers or credentials were required to access the /api endpoint.
The default traefik entrypoint on port 8080 has no authentication configured.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository (traefik/traefik) on the host, checks out the v1.7.34 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
